mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll gains (PAYEMS) indicate robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high PAYEMS periods fall below 1% or show negative correlation with PAYEMS growth (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 2% signals strengthening manufacturing activity, which drives equity market optimism and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth exceeds 2% but SPX 20-day returns are negative or below 0.5% in 60%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) over the past 6 months indicates sustained economic demand, which supports corporate profitability and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% YoY but SPX 60-day returns are negative or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling FEDFUNDS and stable CPIAUCSL indicates accommodative policy without inflationary pressure, creating favorable conditions for equity market gains over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS falls and CPIAUCSL remains stable but SPX 20-day returns are negative or IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: High FEDFUNDS rates during a tightening cycle signal reduced liquidity, increasing discount rates and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive despite FEDFUNDS > 4.5% in 60%+ of cases or IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for residential construction, which boosts related industries and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST growth exceeds 5% MoM but SPX 5-day returns are negative or IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5% signals tight labor markets that drive wage growth and corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE < 5% but SPX 20-day returns are negative or IC < 0.03.
